description: A U.S. Department of Labor designed graphic on a near-black background. The upper portion shows a photograph of President Donald Trump seated at the Resolute Desk in the Oval Office, head bowed as he signs a document; behind him are the gold drapes, military service flags (with eagle finials), and a framed historical illustration on the wall. Overlaid in white serif type (italic 'Protecting' above bold 'AMERICAN JOBS') is the slogan 'Protecting AMERICAN JOBS'. Below is a white rounded-rectangle card styled as a news excerpt with the bold 'Newsweek' wordmark at top, followed by black body text: 'Project Firewall marks a turning point in how the United States polices high-skilled immigration. For the first time, the Labor Department can launch its own investigations into H-1B visa use without waiting for worker complaints, shifting the system from reactive to proactive enforcement. ... Announced last month, Project Firewall represents the first time in more than three decades that a U.S. labor secretary has pledged to personally certify investigations into suspected H-1B and Labor Condition Application (LCA) violations.' The technique pairs a presidential 'action' photo with a mainstream-outlet quote to give authority and credibility to the H-1B enforcement initiative ('Project Firewall'). Target audience: American workers and the administration's base, reinforcing the 'Americans First' jobs message.
notable_text: Protecting AMERICAN JOBS | Newsweek: Project Firewall marks a turning point in how the United States polices high-skilled immigration... the first time in more than three decades that a U.S. labor secretary has pledged to personally certify investigations into suspected H-1B and Labor Condition Application (LCA) violations.
